nodes.name: Copywriting AI Agent

parameters.text:
=You are an expert in SEO content writing.

Your mission is to create, publish, and notify about a search engine optimized article for a blog focused on artificial intelligence. Follow the steps below: {{ $('Format Research Output').item.json.research }}

1. **Write an SEO-optimized article with a maximum of 20 lines** based on the provided information:
   - Structure the article with a catchy **H1 title**, one or two **H2 subtitles**, and a professional yet accessible tone.
   - Extract and include relevant keywords from the data.
   - Optimize for readability: short sentences, clear paragraphs, and a CTA if relevant.
   - Do not exceed 20 lines of content.

2. **Publish the article on WordPress**, including:
   - The **title** as the article's headline
   - The **SEO content** as the body

3. **Send an email** to my address : {{ $json.emailAddress }} containing:
   - The article's title
   - The **URL** of the published article on WordPress

4. **Retrieve the list of available Slack tools first** using “Slack Tools”.
   - Then, send a notification on Slack that the article has been published, including:
     - The article title
     - The article link
     - Slack channel ID: {{ $json.slackChannelId }}

5. **Retrieve the list of available Notion tools first** using “Notion Tools”.
   Then, **add a new entry to my Notion database** (ID: {{ $json.notionDatabaseId }}) with the following fields:
   - The 'Name' column is of type 'title'  → {{ $('Start with Research Query Submission').item.json['Topic or Question'] }}
   The 'Subject' column is of type 'rich_text' → [the article's headline]
   - The 'Content'column is of type 'rich_text' → [The SEO content]
   - The 'URL' column is of type 'URL': → [The article link]
   - The 'Status' column is of type 'select' → Select: `publish`

Important: Ensure that each step is successfully completed **before proceeding to the next**.

parameters.content:
## Intro Sticky 
🔁 **This workflow automates the full cycle of SEO blog content creation** — from live topic research using Perplexity to blog publishing on WordPress, Slack/Gmail notifications, and Notion logging.

## Workflow Configuration Panel
🛠️ **Set your variables here** (email, Slack, Notion, OpenAI model)

## Perplexity Section
🌐 Calls Perplexity API to get fresh research based on a form input.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: mcp Client  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Start with Research Query Submission".
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Perplexity Research" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "When chat message received" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Slack-List" node (n8n-nodes-mcp.mcpClientTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Copywriting AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Publish Article to WordPress" node (n8n-nodes-base.wordpressTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Email Notification" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Notify Slack Channel" node (n8n-nodes-mcp.mcpClientTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Generate SEO Blog Content (GPT-4o)" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Notion-List" node (n8n-nodes-mcp.mcpClientTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Insert Article in Notion" node (n8n-nodes-mcp.mcpClientTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Format Research Output".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Edit Workflow Variables".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
